title: unique if violation fixed by #6450
body:
I have a code snippet that fails consistently on verilator 5.032 and earlier versions, however it appears to have been fixed by the commit mentioned in #6450 as released in 5.042.

This is the unique0 code snippet:

```
        unique0 if (A) begin
            signal = something;
        end else if (B) begin
            signal = something;
        end else if (C) begin
            signal = something;
        end
```

Specifically, verilator reports a statement violation when two of the `if` branches conditions (e.g. B and C) become true (mutually exclusively) in back-to-back cycles: checking with other commercial simulators, there appears to be no glitches in the related signals concurring to A, B, C.

Since the commit mentioned above (57c82b9c34e3f5cb5963abe668919735887d85c2) specifically refers to empty `else` conditions which doesn't appear to be the case in my code, I am opening this for extra zealousness in case it's still worth being taken a look, or if effectively the fix is wider than its commit message suggests.
